settings.title: How to Read a Wine List Without the Anxiety
settings.excerpt: A few small habits make ordering far less stressful, and none of them involve memorizing regions.
settings.body:
People tell us all the time that wine lists make them nervous. The good news is that you do not need to know much to order well.

Tell us two words. Something like 'bright and light' or 'rich and savory' gives us more to work with than a grape name ever could.

Name a price by pointing. Point at a number on the list instead of saying it out loud. We will land near it, no awkwardness.

Trust the by-the-glass section. Those are the bottles we are most excited about right now, which is why they are open.
